_note: Edit this file to add, remove, or adjust rules. Reload the app to pick up changes. Each rule needs: id, name, severity (severe|structural|structural-soft), pattern (JS regex string), flags, message. severe + structural feed the lexical density metric; structural-soft are noisier shape/structure signals that are flagged but EXCLUDED from density.

rules.name: Meta-Announcement
rules.pattern: \b(delve into|let['']s break it down|it is important to note that|it['']s worth noting that|bear in mind that|let['']s dive in|needless to say|it goes without saying|with that in mind|that being said|in this context|in this regard|suffice it to say|without further ado)\b
rules.message: AI throat clearing. Delete the modifier and state the assertion directly.

rules.name: Grandiosity Puffery
rules.pattern: \b(testament to|vibrant tapestry|beacon of hope|poignant reminder|intricate dance|profoundly intertwined|game.changing|groundbreaking|revolutionary|transformative|unprecedented|paradigm shift|cutting.edge|at the forefront|rich tapestry|complex (landscape|ecosystem))\b
rules.message: Artificial inflation of scope. Replace with clear, unembellished nouns.

rules.name: Section Sign
rules.message: The § character is rare in ordinary prose and often leaked LLM section-referencing. NOTE: normal in legal/academic citation — not a tell there.

rules.name: PNAS Corpus Markers
rules.message: High-probability LLM padding term used to mimic intellectual weight.

rules.name: Formulaic Transition
rules.pattern: ^(Furthermore|Moreover|Additionally|In addition|Consequently|Therefore|Nevertheless|Nonetheless|In conclusion|In summary|To summarize|As a result|In other words|On the other hand|It is worth noting|It should be noted)[,\s]
rules.message: Sentence-opening transition formula. AI uses these as structural glue; human writers vary or cut them.

rules.name: Hedge Stack
rules.pattern: \b(may potentially|could possibly|might perhaps|seems to suggest|appears to indicate|tend to often)\b
rules.message: Doubled hedge. Pick one qualifier or drop both and assert directly.

rules.name: Symmetrical Contrast
rules.message: Manufactured binary symmetry. Human speech drifts and digresses; it doesn't mirror.

rules.name: Sweeping Range
rules.message: ‘From X to Y’ sweep. AI uses these to gesture at breadth; usually padding. Name the specific thing instead.

rules.name: Antithesis Framing
rules.message: Manufactured ‘it’s not about X, it’s about Y’ contrast. State the point directly.

rules.name: Wrap-Up Opener
rules.pattern: ^(Ultimately|Overall|At the end of the day|When all is said and done|In the end|All in all|Taken together)[,\s]
rules.message: Formulaic conclusion opener. Cut it or replace with a concrete closing line.
